title: ADK-403: Custom A2UI Component Development
description: Build and register bespoke client-side React widgets and charts that compile into sandboxed A2UI rendering frames.

highlights.title: React Extensions
highlights.description: Author custom React UI templates using strict JSON Schema properties to accept variables from agents.

highlights.title: Data Charting
highlights.description: Integrate visualization charting libraries to represent compound table columns graphically.

highlights.title: Event Callbacks
highlights.description: Define custom callback parameters to send user interaction variables back to the active agent run.

modules.title: 1. Understanding React rendering rules in A2UI

blocks.content: A2UI React Rendering Rules

A2UI is a declarative UI frame. It prevents custom Javascript execution inside templates to block XSS vector attacks. If you build custom components, they must compile down to a static component list and accept variables strictly through properties.

blocks.title: Security Boundary
blocks.content: Components cannot access global `window` or `document` variables. They communicate with the parent frame using serialized state updates.
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: Why does A2UI restrict raw JavaScript execution inside custom component definitions?
- A) To speed up React compiling.
- B) To mitigate XSS vulnerabilities and maintain a secure sandboxed UI frame.
- C) To support older browser versions.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Sandboxing prevents malicious code execution.
Creating custom React components
To add a component, you define its UI structure using React and register its schema so the agent can discover it.
React Card Component Example
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: How are status styles applied dynamically in the badge component above?
- A) Via global CSS overrides
- B) By evaluating status properties to set conditional class string names
- C) Using inline compiled canvas elements

Feedback: Option B is correct. Conditionals adjust style classes dynamically.

modules.title: 3. Binding client-side interactivity and event loops

blocks.content: Client-Side Callbacks
When a user clicks a button inside your custom component, the event must be serialized and passed back to the agent session to continue execution.
Interactive Button Code

blocks.title: Serialization Limits
blocks.content: Callback parameters must be plain JSON objects. Trying to pass complex DOM events or functions will fail serialization.
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: What type of data payloads are allowed in A2UI action triggers?
- A) Raw HTML strings
- B) Plain, JSON-serializable dictionaries
- C) Active Javascript event handlers

Feedback: Option B is correct. Serialization limits payloads to plain JSON.

modules.title: 4. Designing rich file uploads and interactive charts

blocks.content: Data Visualization Components
A2UI includes charting widgets out of the box, allowing agents to present telemetry, token consumption logs, or spreadsheet data to users.
A2UI Chart Schema Payload
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: How does an agent render a line chart inside A2UI?
- A) By generating a PNG image file.
- B) By returning a structured 'chart' schema with labels and values.
- C) By writing CSS templates.

Feedback: Option B is correct. The framework parses schemas to render interactive widgets.

modules.title: 5. Lab: Implementing a real-time data visualization card

blocks.content: Lab: Implementing a Real-Time Data Visualization Card
In this lab, you will define a custom schema block for an agent. The agent will return this schema to display sales metrics.
Step 1: Write the Schema Response
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: What is the main wrapper key for layouts returned in A2UI?
- A) root-layout
- B) a2ui-layout
- C) canvas-frame

Feedback: Option B is correct. a2ui-layout starts layout definitions.
